what_it_does: A full-screen modal image editor with three modes (Adjust, Crop & rotate, AI edit). Adjust applies CSS filters (brightness/contrast/saturation/hue) plus seven named filter presets; Crop offers draggable-corner crop, aspect-ratio presets, and 90deg/fine rotation plus flips; AI edit sends the baked image to OpenAI via CV_AI and shows returned variants. A canvas 'bake' pipeline composites crop+rotation+flip+filter into a JPEG data URL passed to onSave.
observations: FILTERS array defines seven named presets (Original, Warm paper, Cool dawn, Bronze line, Gold-cast, Mono ink, Soft fade) as CSS filter strings.
bake() builds a rotation-aware target canvas (swaps width/height on 90/270deg), applies crop source-rect, ctx.filter = filterCss, flips via ctx.scale, exports image/jpeg at 0.92 quality.
Crop overlay implemented with clipPath polygon mask and four draggable corner handles + a move region, with clamp()-bounded drag math in image-normalized 0..1 coordinates.
aspectCrop(ratio) computes a centered crop for a target aspect ratio against the image's natural aspect.
runAiEdit() guards on window.cvOpenAI.isConfigured(), bakes the image to a blob, then calls window.CV_AI.resolveProvider('openai-image').editImage({prompt, imageBlob}).
Uses CvIcon, ViShape, and a local Slider subcomponent; mode tabs render different side panels.
Error handling uses window.dsaToast?.() for user-facing failures; save() swallows the bake error into a toast.
Comment notes it is used by 'every imagery surface (System, Projects, AI Studio)'.
standouts: Routes AI image edits through CV_AI.resolveProvider('openai-image').editImage rather than calling OpenAI directly — honors the project's single-AI-registry rule.
The filter presets are brand-flavored (Warm paper, Bronze line, Gold-cast) tying the editor to the ConceptV palette identity.
Rotation-aware bake canvas correctly handles quarter-turn dimension swapping.
conventions: Follows browser-React no-build pattern (suffixed React hook aliases useState_ed etc., window attach).
Uses .cv-ed-* and .dsa-btn class vocabulary; AI calls go through CV_AI per the project's AI-registry non-negotiable.
AI configured-check uses window.cvOpenAI.isConfigured() directly (a global) for gating, while the actual edit goes through CV_AI.
concerns: bake() applies ctx.filter for canvas compositing — CSS filter support on canvas 2D contexts is uneven across browsers, so baked output may diverge from the on-screen preview on some browsers (file-local risk, unverified here).
The hue 'filter' preset combination concatenates preset css with brightness/contrast/etc — ordering/interaction of doubled sepia/saturate functions could compound unexpectedly.
notes: The shared modal image editor for all imagery surfaces — local canvas adjust/crop/rotate plus an OpenAI-backed AI edit tab, all baked through one canvas pipeline.
questions: Why does the AI configured-check use window.cvOpenAI.isConfigured() directly while the edit call routes through CV_AI.resolveProvider — are these two views of the same provider config?
What shape does editImage return (the code maps r.src), and where does onSave's data URL get stored?
purpose_vs_actual: Match — header comment describes a modal canvas editor doing crop/adjust/filter/rotate plus OpenAI routing, which the code implements.
apparent_themes: One shared component reused across many surfaces (System, Projects, AI Studio)
AI routed through the single registry (CV_AI), never the raw provider
Brand identity expressed through named filter presets
Canvas-based deterministic baking of a visual edit pipeline
intention_signals: Aims to give every imagery surface one consistent, brand-flavored editor that does both manual edits and AI edits, with the AI path disciplined through the central registry rather than ad-hoc provider calls.
novelty: Combines a self-contained canvas crop/rotate/filter pipeline with an AI-edit tab in one modal, with brand-named filter presets and registry-routed image AI.
contribution: Contributes the reusable full-modal image editing surface — the manual canvas adjust/crop/rotate/bake pipeline and the registry-routed AI-edit tab — that imagery features across the app share.
summary_for_embedding: Full-screen React modal image editor with adjust (CSS filters + presets), crop/rotate/flip, and an OpenAI-via-CV_AI edit tab, baking edits through a canvas to a JPEG data URL.
intention_for_embedding: To provide one shared, brand-styled image editor for all imagery surfaces, doing manual canvas edits and registry-routed AI edits.
